key_insight: SGD with large learning rates can self-stabilize at the edge of stability, alternating between oscillatory and stable phases while still converging in a best-iterate sense.
engineering_relevance: This suggests practitioners can use larger stepsizes than classical theory allows, potentially speeding training without losing convergence guarantees in multiclass classification models.
method: Analyze SGD dynamics for multiclass cross-entropy under large learning rates.
Prove fixed-time return to stability after edge-of-stability excursions.
Establish best-iterate convergence for linear classifiers and two-layer neural networks.
evidence_signal: The abstract says experiments validate the theory and demonstrate the benefits of SGD in the large-stepsize regime.
watch_item: The guarantees are shown for specific model classes and loss setup, so extending the result to broader architectures or training pipelines may require extra analysis.
paper_title: SGD at the Edge of Stability: Stochastic Stabilization with Large Learning Rates
